design.method: For each of 10 verifiable questions (deadline 2026-06-20), generate TWO independent forecasts: (a) NAIVE = reference class + base rate only, no domain analysis, (b) SWARM = pre-registered, multi-domain decomposition, EAD, explicit falsification. Compare Brier scores on resolution.
design.hypothesis: Swarm-method Brier score at least 0.05 lower than naive across 10 questions
design.falsification: No significant difference (p > 0.05) or swarm-method WORSE than naive
design.power_analysis: 10 paired questions gives ~60% power to detect 0.05 Brier difference at alpha=0.05 (paired t-test). Marginal — will need follow-up if null result.
bias_controls: Naive forecast generated FIRST to prevent anchoring
No revision of naive after swarm forecast generated
All questions chosen for June 2026 resolution to avoid selective timing

questions.question: Will SPY close below $600 on 2026-06-20?
questions.resolution_source: Yahoo Finance SPY closing price on 2026-06-20

naive.reasoning: SPY has closed below its level from 3 months prior in ~30-40% of rolling 3-month windows since 2000. Current SPY ~560. $600 is ~7% above. Base rate ~35%.

swarm.reasoning: CAPE=39 (97th percentile mean reversion). Momentum negative (below 50/100/200-day MAs). Tariff escalation as prisoner's dilemma. Fed paralyzed. 1973-74 analog: drawdown takes 18 months, we're 2 months in.
swarm.ead_checkpoint: Check SPY on 2026-04-20; if above $620, revise to 0.40

questions.question: Will the Fed cut the federal funds rate by at least 25bp before 2026-06-20?
questions.resolution_source: Federal Reserve press release after FOMC meetings

naive.reasoning: Fed has cut at least once within 3-month windows in ~40% of periods with slowing growth + above-target inflation (post-1990).

swarm.reasoning: Dual mandate conflict = hold. Fed credibility asset at risk. 1970s premature cut analog. Oil above $90 = supply shock = can't cut into it. Institutional hawkish-sticky bias from 2021-22 trauma.
swarm.ead_checkpoint: Check after May 6-7 FOMC; if hold confirmed, revise to 0.15

questions.question: Will Bitcoin trade above $100,000 at any point before 2026-06-20?

naive.reasoning: BTC has rallied >45% from local low within 3 months in ~40-50% of post-halving years. Currently ~$84K. Needs ~19% rally.

swarm.reasoning: Risk-off suppresses speculative assets. BTC-QQQ correlation 0.6+. Momentum negative (below 200-day MA). Fear regime duration > greed. Real rates high = opportunity cost of non-yielding assets.
swarm.ead_checkpoint: If BTC breaks above $95K before April 15, revise to 0.55

questions.question: Will US unemployment rate (BLS U-3) exceed 4.5% in any monthly report released before 2026-06-20?

naive.reasoning: U-3 has risen from ~4.1% to >4.5% within 3 months in ~20-30% of periods with softening labor + positive GDP.

swarm.reasoning: Tariff-driven disruption hits employment with 3-6 month lag. Unemployment follows regime-switching dynamics (Sahm rule near trigger). 2001/2008: acceleration preceded by 'resilient' data. Labor is lagging indicator — damage baked in.
swarm.ead_checkpoint: Check April BLS (early May); if U-3 > 4.3%, revise to 0.45

questions.question: Will WTI crude oil trade above $100/barrel at any point before 2026-06-20?
questions.resolution_source: NYMEX WTI front-month contract daily close

naive.reasoning: WTI has breached $100 in ~35-45% of 3-month windows during active Middle East conflicts since 1990. Currently ~$95.

swarm.reasoning: Hormuz closure removes ~20% global crude transit. Inventory drawdowns ~2-3mbpd with no replacement. Historical analogs (1973, 1990, 2022) show larger moves. Demand destruction threshold ~$120-130. SPR depleted. OPEC+ spare capacity shrunk.
swarm.ead_checkpoint: If Hormuz reopens by April 15, revise to 0.15

questions.question: Will OpenAI publicly release or announce GPT-5 (or equivalent next-gen model) before 2026-06-20?
questions.resolution_source: Official OpenAI blog post or press release

naive.reasoning: Major AI labs release next-gen models within 12-18 months of previous gen ~50% of the time. 12+ months since last major release.

swarm.reasoning: Competitive pressure (Claude 4.5/4.6, Gemini 2, Llama 4). $6.5B funding requires visible progress. Release cadence accelerating (GPT-3→4: 2.5yr; 4→4o: 14mo; 4o→o1: 4mo). Compute scaling continues.
swarm.falsification: No announcement by June 20 AND no credible leaks
swarm.ead_checkpoint: If no signals by May 1, revise to 0.35

questions.question: Will the US 10-year Treasury yield fall below 4.0% at any point before 2026-06-20?

naive.reasoning: 10Y yield has moved below a level 40bp below current in ~40-50% of 3-month windows during growth scares since 2010. Currently ~4.35%.

swarm.reasoning: Stagflation = yields rise not fall (1970s: 6%→10%). $36T debt + fiscal deficits = supply pressure. Fed holding means short end anchored. Japan/China reducing holdings. Flight-to-safety partially offset by inflation expectations.
swarm.ead_checkpoint: If 10Y hits 4.5%+ by April, revise to 0.25

questions.question: Will NVIDIA (NVDA) stock close above $130 on 2026-06-20?
questions.resolution_source: Yahoo Finance NVDA closing price on 2026-06-20

naive.reasoning: Large-cap tech >20% off ATH recovers to within 10% of prior level within 3 months ~40-50% of the time. NVDA ~$115, $130 is ~13% from current.

swarm.reasoning: P/E ~55 still elevated. China chip restrictions escalating (DeepSeek showed less NVDA dependence). AI capex cycle may be peaking. NVDA as sentiment proxy absorbs narrative shift from euphoria to skepticism. Below all major MAs.
swarm.ead_checkpoint: NVDA earnings (late May); if revenue beat + guidance up, revise to 0.55

questions.question: Will there be a documented military engagement between Chinese and Taiwanese forces before 2026-06-20?
questions.resolution_source: Reuters/AP/BBC report of direct military engagement (not exercises/drills)

naive.reasoning: 0 direct engagements since 1979 despite ~50 periods of elevated tension. Extreme base rate: ~3-5% in any 3-month high-tension window.

swarm.reasoning: Iterated deterrence game with credible mutual destruction (economic). Gray-zone pattern held 75+ years. US treaty ambiguity deters. China economy struggling — sanctions cost too high. Xi's risk profile is calculated, not impulsive. Hormuz already strains system.
swarm.falsification: Any kinetic engagement reported by 2+ wire services
swarm.ead_checkpoint: N/A — low probability. Monitor via news.

questions.question: Will US CPI year-over-year exceed 4.0% in any monthly report released before 2026-06-20?

naive.reasoning: CPI has risen >1pp from ~2.8% to >4% within 3 months in ~25-35% of periods with active supply shocks (1973, 1979, 1990, 2022).

swarm.reasoning: Oil above $90 feeds through with 2-3 month lag. Tariff-driven core goods inflation compounds. 1973 analog: 3%→8% in 12 months. 2022: 7%→9% in 3 months. Consumer inflation expectations already rising (Michigan survey). Behavioral anchoring shifts discontinuously.
swarm.ead_checkpoint: April CPI (mid-May); if below 3.0%, revise to 0.10

methodology_notes.scoring_plan: On resolution: Brier = (prob - outcome)^2 per forecast. Paired t-test on 10 pairs. Also: calibration, resolution, reliability decomposition.
methodology_notes.key_test: Is multi-domain analysis adding SIGNAL or just COMPLEXITY? Bidirectional divergence (5 up, 4 down, 1 same) is a positive sign — method adds information not bias.

expect: Swarm-method forecasts achieve Brier >=0.05 lower than naive across 10 questions. Method transfer is real.
actual: EXPERIMENT REGISTERED. 10 paired forecasts. Mean naive 0.36, swarm 0.38. Divergence bidirectional (8.5pp avg). Largest: Q05 oil (+20pp). Resolution: June 20, 2026.
diff: Design achieved as expected. Surprising: swarm method is NOT systematically directional — divergences are bidirectional, suggesting information not bias. Concern: small average divergence (8.5pp) may not produce detectable Brier improvement at n=10.
